back_translation: At that time, Herod the tetrarch heard reports about Jesus,
and said to his servants: 'This is John the Baptizer; he has risen from the dead, and for that reason miraculous powers are at work in him.'
For previously, Herod had arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison, on account of Herodias, the wife of Philip his brother.
Because John had said to Herod: 'It is not lawful for you to have her as your wife.'
Although Herod wanted to kill John, he feared the crowd, because they regarded John as a prophet.
But when Herod's birthday came, the daughter of Herodias danced in front of the guests, and pleased Herod.
Therefore he promised her with an oath to give whatever she asked.
She, being prompted by her mother in advance, said: 'Give me the head of John the Baptizer on a platter here.'
The king was grieved, but on account of his oath and the guests at the table, he ordered it to be done as she asked.
So he sent and beheaded John in the prison.
John's head was brought on a platter and given to the young woman, and she brought it to her mother.
John's disciples came to receive the body for burial, and then went and informed Jesus.
When Jesus heard about it, He withdrew from there by boat to a deserted place privately. But the crowds heard the news and followed Him by land from the cities.
When He had come ashore, He saw a great crowd; He was moved with compassion, and healed their sick.
When evening came, the disciples came to Him and said: 'This is a deserted place, and the hour is now late; send the crowds away, so that they may go and buy food for themselves in the villages.'
Jesus answered them: 'They do not need to go anywhere. You yourselves give them something to eat.'
They said to Him: 'Here we have only five loaves of bread and two fish.'
He said: 'Bring them to me here.'

He commanded the crowd to sit down on the grass, then took the five loaves of bread and the two fish, looked up to heaven, gave thanks, broke the bread, and gave it to the disciples; the disciples gave it to the crowd.
Everyone ate until they were full, and the disciples gathered up the leftover pieces of bread, twelve baskets full.
Those who ate were about five thousand men, not counting women and children.
Immediately He compelled the disciples to get into the boat and go ahead of Him to the other side, while He sent the crowds away.
After He had sent the crowds away, He went up on the mountain alone to pray. When evening came, He was there alone.
Now the boat was already many stadia away from the shore, beaten by the waves because the wind was contrary.
In the fourth watch of the night, Jesus came to them, walking on the surface of the sea.
When the disciples saw Him walking on the sea, they were terrified and said: 'A ghost!' and cried out in fear.
But immediately Jesus said to them: 'Take heart, it is I; do not be afraid.'
Peter answered Him: 'Lord, if it is truly You, command me to come to You on the water.'
He said: 'Come.' So Peter got out of the boat, walked on the water, and went toward Jesus.
But when Peter saw the strong wind, he was afraid; and beginning to sink, he cried out: 'Lord, save me!'
Immediately Jesus reached out His hand and caught Peter, and said to him: 'You of little faith, why did you doubt?'
When He and Peter got into the boat, the wind ceased.
Those in the boat worshiped Him, saying: 'Truly You are the Son of God.'
When they had crossed over, they came to land in the region of Gennesaret.
When the people of that area recognized Him, they sent word throughout the surrounding region, and brought all the sick to Him.
They begged Him to allow them only to touch the hem of His garment, and all who touched it were healed.
